perturbation.original_text: 5.1 License Fees. In consideration of the licenses granted and other undertakings by Licensor hereunder, Licensee shall pay Licensor a License Fee in the amount of two hundred thousand dollars (US$200,000) ("License Fee"). The License Fee shall be due and payable as follows: (a) US$50,000 upon signing the Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") between the Parties (provided, however, that Licensor hereby acknowledges receipt of this portion of the License Fee); (b) US$50,000 upon signing of this Agreement; and (c) US$100,000 upon on or before March 28, 2014. The License Fees are non-cancellable and non-refundable.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 License Fees. In consideration of the licenses granted and other undertakings by Licensor hereunder, Licensee shall pay Licensor a License Fee in the amount of two hundred thousand dollars (US$200,000) ("License Fee"). The License Fee shall be due and payable as follows: (a) US$50,000 upon signing the Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") between the Parties (provided, however, that Licensor hereby acknowledges receipt of this portion of the License Fee); (b) US$50,000 upon signing of this Agreement; and (c) US$100,000 upon on or before April 15, 2014. The License Fees are non-cancellable and non-refundable.
perturbation.explanation: The change in the final payment date from March 28, 2014, to April 15, 2014, creates an in-text contradiction with Section 5.3(b)(ii), which discusses interest on past due amounts if License Fees or Royalties are more than ten (10) days past due. The contradiction lies in the uncertainty of when the payment is officially late, since one section now indicates April 15th but the other references a time frame, where it may be considered late if paid after March 28th.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 Reports and Payment (a) Not later than the fifteenth (15th) calendar day of each calendar quarter (or the first business day thereafter), Licensee shall deliver to Licensor (i) a report accurately showing Gross Revenues of the Licensee for the previous quarter, the number of Subscribers on the last day of such previous quarter and the amount of Royalties due thereon, and (ii) payment of the Royalties payable for such previous quarterbased on the Subscriber information as reflected in the report. All payments under this Agreement shall be made in US dollars. (b) If Licensee fails to make any payment when due: (i)Licensor shall have the right to terminate this Agreement and all licenses granted hereunder as provided in Section 6.2; and] (ii)If any License Fees or Royalties are more than ten (10) days past due, Licensor may assess interest on the past due amount at the rate of one and a half percent (1.5%) per month or, if lower, the highest rate permitted under applicable Law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the final payment date from March 28, 2014, to April 15, 2014. This creates a direct conflict with Section 5.3(b)(ii), which states that interest may be assessed if License Fees are more than ten (10) days past due. The contradiction is in the ambiguity of when a payment is officially late, as one section now indicates April 15th, while 5.3(b)(ii) calculates lateness based on a ten-day window, creating confusion and potential dispute over the payment due date and associated penalties, hence is a strong contradiction as per the definition: Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: Inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Term. The initial term of this Agreement commences as of the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier pursuant to any express provision of this Agreement, shall continue until five (5) years following the Effective Date (the "Initial Term"). Thereafter, this Agreement shall renew automatically for one (1) additional three (3) year period (the "Renewal Term" and collectively, together with the Initial Term, the "Term") unless either party provides the other with written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days before the expiration of the Initial Term].
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Term. The initial term of this Agreement commences as of the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier pursuant to any express provision of this Agreement, shall continue until five (5) years following the Effective Date (the "Initial Term"). Thereafter, this Agreement shall renew automatically for one (1) additional five (5) year period (the "Renewal Term" and collectively, together with the Initial Term, the "Term") unless either party provides the other with written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days before the expiration of the Initial Term].
perturbation.explanation: The change in the renewal term from three (3) years to five (5) years creates an in-text contradiction because the contract provides that if Licensor terminates the contract due to Licensee breach of contract, all License fees that would have been payable through the end of the then current term had the agreement not been terminated early become immediately due and payable. As, such if such termiation happens in the initial term, it becomes unclear as to if the license fees would have to accounted from the intial 3 year renewal or the modified 5 year renewal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 Effect of Expiration or Termination. Upon any expiration or termination of this Agreement: (a) All licenses granted under this Agreement shall also terminate, and Licensee shall immediately delete from its systems and servers all Licensed Content, Licensor's Marks and any other materials provided by Licensor. Upon Licensor's written request, Licensee shall promptly provide Licensor with written certification of such deletion. (b) Licensee shall promptly pay all unpaid License Fees that relate to the period prior to the effective date of expiration or termination. (c) If termination is by Licensor pursuant to Section 6.2(a), all License Fees that would have been payable through the end of the then- current Term had the Agreement not been terminated early shall become immediately due and payable. (d) If termination is by Licensee pursuant to Section 6.2(a), Licensee shall be relieved of any obligation to pay License Fees that relate to the period after the effective date of termination. (e) Each party shall (i) return to the other party all documents and tangible materials (and any copies) containing, reflecting, incorporating or based on the other party's Confidential Information, (ii) permanently erase all of the other party's Confidential Information from its computer systems and (iii) certify in writing to the other party that it has complied with the requirements of this Section 6.3(e).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal term from three (3) years to five (5) years. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because Section 6.3(c) states that if the agreement is terminated early due to Licensee breach, all License Fees that would have been payable through the end of the then-current Term become immediately due. The altered renewal period introduces uncertainty as to whether the outstanding fees are calculated using the original three-year renewal or the changed five-year renewal term, creating an inconsistency regarding the amount owed. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations, specifically the obligation to pay license fees upon early termination.

perturbation.original_text: 11.12 Dispute Resolution. In the event of a dispute arising out of this Agreement, the parties shall first negotiate in good faith in an effort to reach a settlement of the dispute. If having negotiated in good faith, the parties are unable to resolve their dispute, the parties shall submit the dispute to binding arbitration by a single arbitrator. The arbitration shall be governed by the Commercial Rules of Arbitration of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitration shall take place at an agreed location, or if the parties cannot agree on a venue in Reno, Nevada or Vancouver, BC, as determined by the flip of a coin. The arbitrator shall have jurisdiction over the conduct of discovery prior to the hearing. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Section 11.13 shall be construed to prohibit either party from seeking appropriate injunctive or other equitable relief in a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.12 Dispute Resolution. In the event of a dispute arising out of this Agreement, the parties shall first negotiate in good faith in an effort to reach a settlement of the dispute. If having negotiated in good faith, the parties are unable to resolve their dispute, the parties shall submit the dispute to non-binding mediation by a mutually agreed mediator. If mediation fails to resolve the dispute within thirty (30) days, either party may then pursue litigation in a court of competent jurisdiction. The mediation shall be governed by the Model Standards of Conduct for Mediators jointly issued by the American Arbitration Association, the American Bar Association, and the Association for Conflict Resolution. The mediation shall take place at an agreed location. Each party shall bear its own costs associated with the mediation. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Section 11.13 shall be construed to prohibit either party from seeking appropriate injunctive or other equitable relief in a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The change from binding arbitration to non-binding mediation directly contradicts the language in Section 11.13 Attorneys' Fees, which anticipates a legal proceeding (action, suit, or other legal or administrative proceeding). Since mediation is not a legal proceeding, there is an in-text contradiction because if the matter is settled through mediation, there is no 'prevailing party' and, therefore, no basis for awarding attorneys' fees and court costs under the altered dispute resolution process
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.13 Attorneys' Fees. In the event that any action, suit, or other legal or administrative proceeding is instituted or commenced by either party hereto against the other party arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and court costs from the non-prevailing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates binding arbitration, which inherently involves a legal proceeding leading to a 'prevailing party.' Section 11.13 specifies that the prevailing party in any 'action, suit, or other legal or administrative proceeding' arising from the agreement is entitled to attorneys' fees and court costs. The changed text replaces binding arbitration with non-binding mediation. Because mediation does not result in a binding legal outcome or a 'prevailing party' in the legal sense, the mechanism for awarding attorneys' fees as outlined in Section 11.13 is removed, which creates an in-text contradiction. This inconsistency leads to uncertainty about how disputes are resolved and whether attorneys' fees are recoverable. Therefore, the altered dispute resolution process directly contradicts Section 11.13.
